commonIssues:
Due to the mandatory Aadhaar-enabled Public Distribution System (e-POS machines), many beneficiaries—particularly daily wage labourers and the elderly—face issues because their fingerprints do not match due to manual labour or aging.

How to fix:

  • Request the FPS dealer to authenticate via the mobile OTP method, or use the iris scanner if the dealer's e-POS machine is equipped with one.
  • Visit an Aadhaar enrollment center to update your biometrics to ensure a smoother matching process in the future.

A significant issue is the requirement to link ration cards with Aadhaar through e-KYC. Technical glitches, server down-time, or slow connectivity at fair price shops can cause long delays and prevent people from getting their monthly rations.

How to fix:

  • Try visiting the Fair Price Shop during off-peak hours (early morning) when server loads are generally lighter.
  • You can independently complete the e-KYC of all family members using the MP Ration Mitra App to avoid FPS dependency.

Many families report that their names have been mistakenly deleted from the ration list during state-wide cleaning exercises aimed at removing ineligible or deceased beneficiaries.

How to fix:

  • File a re-verification application at your local Panchayat office or Municipal Ward office along with your Aadhaar and a living certificate (or physical presence).
  • Check your current status on the Samagra portal; if your Samagra ID is active, ask the local nodal officer to manually re-link it to the PDS database.

In a surprising twist, reports have shown that in some instances, dead individuals continued to receive rations, while living, eligible beneficiaries struggled to receive theirs due to technical errors, such as being erroneously marked as dead on the Samagra Portal.

How to fix:

  • If marked dead erroneously, immediately visit the Janpad Panchayat (rural) or Zonal Office (urban) and request a "Jivit Praman" (Life Verification) process to update the Samagra ID.
  • Submit a written complaint to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) or raise a grievance on the CM Helpline (181).

Despite the use of electronic weighing machines, some beneficiaries complain that dealers give less than the allocated amount of food grains or demand extra payment, particularly in rural or remote areas.

How to fix:

  • Demand the printed receipt generated by the e-POS machine before leaving the shop. Do not pay more than the printed amount or accept less weight than printed.
  • Register an instant complaint on the CM Helpline (181) or via the M-Ration Mitra app noting the exact shop number and dealer details.

Some eligible households struggle to get their cards updated to Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or Priority Household (PHH) status, affecting their ability to get high-subsidized food grains.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a valid and recent income certificate showing eligibility for AAY or PHH.
  • Submit a formal application for category upgrading to the District Supply Officer (DSO) or local Tehsildar along with the proof of severe vulnerability (e.g., widowhood, severe disability, or extreme poverty).

Complaints about receiving inferior quality food grains (wheat/rice) or not receiving the full quota of pulses/salt are occasionally reported.

How to fix:

  • Retain a sample of the substandard grain and refuse to accept the allocation in the system.
  • Report the issue directly to the state Food and Civil Supplies grievance portal or the District Collector during public grievance days (Jan Sunwai).
